entries.raw_excerpt:
Avon-by-the-Sea Planning Board Reorganization Meeting Minutes 
February 26, 2026 
Opening Statement read by Chair Ryan.  
Pledge Of Allegiance 
Roll Call  
Present: Chair Ryan, Vice Chair Egan, Kenny, McLaughlin, Mazouat, Child, Kennedy, 
Doran, Attorney Kitrick, Engineer Savacool 
 Absent: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, McGovern, Conaghan, Hardie 
Correspondence: 
• Attorney Kitrick acknowledged the r esignation of Chair Richard Maloney and Mike 
Davey.  
• Attorney Kitrick acknowledged the a ppointment of new Chair/members  and welcomed 
new members to the Board.  
Swearing in of Planning Board appointees for new terms by Attorney Kitrick: 
 Jack Ryan  Chair   1 year  End 12/31/26 
 John Magrini  Class III  1 year  End 12/31/26 (Absent) 
Steve Mazouat  Class IV   4 years  End 12/31/29 
David Conaghan Class IV  4 years  End 12/31/29 (Absent) 
Alexandra Hardie  Alternate #1 (to fill  2 years  End 12/31/26 
unexpired term)    
John Kennedy  Alternate #3  2 years  End 12/31/27 
Kev
entries.source_url: https://www.avonbytheseanj.com/2.26.26 Reorg and Regular Meeting Minutes.pdf?t=202603251615310
entries.summary:
- The board approved resolutions repealing and replacing two chapters related to affordable housing, which may influence future developments and property values.
- A new application for a use variance was presented for a property at 112 Garfield Avenue, focusing on significant renovations, which may impact neighborhood aesthetics and property values.
- Public comments were largely supportive of the proposed renovations at 112 Garfield Avenue, indicating a positive neighborhood sentiment that could enhance local desirability.
- The application for 112 Garfield Avenue has been continued to March 23, 2026, which may delay any changes and their impact on property values in the immediate term.
- Height and coverage variances are being considered, signaling a possible shift in development standards that could affect investment strategies in the area.

entries.raw_excerpt:
Avon-by-the-Sea Planning Board Regular Meeting Agenda  
December 22, 2025 – 7:00 P.M. 
Opening Statement read by Chair Maloney.  
Pledge Of Allegiance 
Roll Call  
Present: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Chair Maloney, Vice Chair Egan, Ryan, 
Davey, Kenny, McLaughin, Mazouat, Conaghan 
 Absent: McGovern, Child, Hardie 
 
Mayor Bonanno announced the resignation of Chair Maloney from the Planning Board. Thanked 
him for his service over the past 25 years. Has been a tremendous asset to the Board and he will 
be missed. 
 
Motion to approve the minutes of the November 24, 2025 Meeting.  
 Motion by Chair Maloney, seconded by Ryan that the minutes be approved.  
 The V ote: 
Aye: Mayor Bonanno, Commissioner Magrini, Chair Maloney, Vice Chair Egan, Ryan, 
Davey, McLaughin, Conaghan 
 No: None 
 Motion passed.  
 
Motion to approve Resolution No. 25-2025 – Loures - 224 Roosevelt Avenue.  
 Motion by Chair Maloney, seconded by Vice Chair Egan that the resolution be approved.  
 The V ot
entries.summary:
- A variance was granted for 100 Norwood Avenue to allow an addition and interior modifications despite setback non-compliance, indicating potential value add for renovated homes.
- The Planning Board approved a project for 319 Sylvania Avenue designed to assist aging residents, showcasing community support for family-oriented developments, which may enhance neighborhood desirability.
- Public comments were mixed for the Cunningham project, with some residents supporting modifications to minimize impact, reflecting ongoing community engagement and potential for future challenges in similar proposals.
- The meeting noted the departure of Chair Maloney, suggesting possible shifts in Board dynamics that could influence future planning decisions.
- Enhanced outdoor amenities were discussed for the Cunningham project, such as relocating an outdoor shower and adding a sidewalk, which could improve overall property appeal in the area.

entries.summary:
- The Planning Board approved two variance requests for residential properties, enhancing the potential for property improvements in the area.
- A new application for a home at 224 Roosevelt Avenue includes plans to address flooding issues and elevate the property, which may positively impact neighborhood desirability.
- A proposed garage apartment addition for the Society of Mount Carmel is currently under review, with concerns about neighborhood character and potential overuse of land being raised by board members.
- Public commentary indicated some neighborhood concerns regarding variances, signaling a level of engagement and possible resistance to new developments.
- The board's decision on the garage apartment will be postponed, allowing for further consideration, which introduces uncertainty into that property's development timeline.

entries.summary:
- A variance was granted for a property at 22 Lakeside Avenue, potentially enhancing value in that area.
- An application for 319 Sylvania Avenue was carried to December with no public opposition, indicating ongoing interest but uncertainty around property changes.
- A new application at 617 Fifth Avenue proposing significant modifications and variances was approved, which suggests improving property appeal and potential marketability.
- A use variance application for 103 Norwood Avenue was also approved, indicating a trend of property enhancements that could positively impact neighborhood desirability.
- Public comments were supportive of multiple projects, suggesting a community favorable to developments that align with existing character, which could stabilize or increase property values.

entries.summary:
- Two use variances were granted for properties at 39 Norwood Avenue and 35 Garfield Avenue, indicating a trend of successful applications for similar requests that may affect local property values.
- A contentious hearing regarding a variance for 319 Sylvania Avenue resulted in significant public opposition, which could signal resistance to future developments on undersized lots in that neighborhood.
- An application for subdividing a former bed and breakfast at 22 Lakeside Avenue into two oversized lots is underway, which could enhance property values if approved, as it aligns with neighbor's existing structures.
- The planning board expressed concerns about overdevelopment of undersized lots, which may impact future zoning decisions and housing density in the area.
- There is ongoing public interest and potential contention surrounding property modifications that affect light, air, and open space, suggesting a need for buyers to be aware of neighborhood dynamics regarding development.

entries.summary:
- A variance was granted for 210 Washington Avenue, potentially enhancing property values in that area, while a variance was denied for 23 Garfield Avenue, indicating stricter enforcement of zoning regulations nearby.
- The application for a use variance at 39 Norwood Avenue was approved, allowing for a 2-family home renovation that aligns with neighborhood character and solidifies the homeowners’ long-term presence in the community.
- A proposed new single-family structure at 35 Garfield Avenue, designed for accessibility due to the owner's medical condition, was also approved, which may positively influence nearby properties' appeal.
- Multiple public comments expressed strong support for both the renovations at 39 Norwood Avenue and the new structure at 35 Garfield Avenue, suggesting a favorable neighborhood sentiment towards investment in property improvements.

entries.summary:
- The Planning Board granted a variance approval for a home at 615 First Avenue, which may enhance property value due to improvements permitted in the area.
- An application for a proposed addition on 210 Washington Avenue, including a full bathroom on the first floor, was approved, reflecting a trend towards modernization and accessibility in home designs.
- An application for enclosing a porch at 23 Garfield Avenue is under review, with mixed neighborhood support, signaling potential aesthetic changes that might impact property desirability in the area. 
- The Board will revisit the Cunningham application on Sylvania Avenue on September 22, 2025, indicating ongoing developments that could influence local property markets.
- Public comments during meetings suggest active community involvement in planning decisions, which may forecast future residential dynamics and property values.

entries.summary:
- A use variance was granted for a property at 314 Norwood Avenue, indicative of flexibility in zoning that could influence nearby property values.
- The Board adopted a Fourth Round Housing Element and Fair Share Plan, emphasizing commitments to affordable housing which may increase long-term demand and value.
- An application for a new deck at 615 First Avenue was approved, indicating minor improvements that could enhance property appeal in that neighborhood.
- The discussion on a Master Plan re-examination suggests potential future zoning changes that may impact property values significantly depending on outcomes.

entries.summary:
- A variance approval was granted for a property on West End Avenue, which could influence nearby property values positively by setting a precedent for approvals.  
- An application for a use variance on Norwood Avenue was carried over, indicating ongoing developments that may affect neighborhood dynamics.  
- Public comments revealed significant community concern regarding the impact of proposed renovations on neighboring properties and overall aesthetics.  
- There is a noted pushback against a proposed pool, with board members expressing concerns about runoff and neighborhood compatibility, hinting at future resistance to this type of project.  
- The meeting included discussions about potential parking and access changes, which could affect property value and desirability in the area depending on the outcomes.
